hypothesis: Implied volatility (as measured by VIX) consistently trades at a premium to realized volatility — sellers of volatility are compensated for providing insurance to hedgers. This structural risk premium is persistent because hedgers (institutions, funds) are willing to overpay for protection, creating a systematic edge for disciplined sellers. The VRP (implied vol - realized vol spread) averages 2-4 vol points historically.
universe: SPX (S&P 500 Index) options and VIX futures. Primary instrument: short SPX put spreads (defined risk). Secondary: VIX futures short when in contango.
entry_logic: VIX futures: go short front-month VIX futures when the VIX term structure is in contango (front < second month), indicating a positive roll yield. SPX options: sell 30-45 DTE put spreads (cash-secured) when VIX > 15 (elevated premium). Target delta ~0.15-0.20 on short leg.
exit_logic: VIX futures: close at 50% profit or roll to next month 5 days before expiry. SPX options: close at 50% profit OR at 21 DTE (whichever comes first) to avoid gamma risk.
position_sizing: Maximum 20% of portfolio in VIX/vol positions at any time. Options: max 30% of buying power used on defined-risk spreads. VIX futures: size to risk 1% of portfolio per contract on a 5-point adverse move.
risk_management: Hard stop: close ALL vol positions if VIX spikes above 40 (extreme fear — risk of unlimited loss on shorts if using undefined risk). Only defined-risk strategies (spreads, not naked shorts). Hedge: maintain a small long VIX call as tail hedge (1-2% of portfolio). No selling vol into earnings weeks or major macro events (FOMC, CPI).
known_failure_modes: VIX spikes (black swan events): COVID crash (Feb-Mar 2020) caused VIX to spike from 15 to 85 in weeks. Short vol positions suffer catastrophic losses if not properly hedged or sized.
Contango-to-backwardation flips: VIX futures roll strategy loses money during backwardation (VIX front > back month), which occurs during sustained high-vol regimes.
Gamma risk near expiry: if holding options into the final 21 DTE without closing, theta/gamma dynamics can quickly reverse profitable positions.
Correlation with equity crash: the VRP strategy loses exactly when portfolios need stability — in market crashes, all vol positions lose simultaneously.
Defined-risk strategies cap upside: put spreads limit premium collected vs. naked puts, reducing edge per trade while maintaining correlation risk.

historical_performance.notes: Excellent in low-vol, trending bull markets (2012-2019 was ideal). Severe drawdown in COVID crash (Mar 2020) even with defined risk. VIX futures roll (short contango) achieves Sharpe ~1.0 standalone. Combined with options premium selling, higher Sharpe but correlated risk. Must use small allocation (< 20% of portfolio) due to tail risk.

references: CBOE VIX White Paper - Understanding the VIX Index
Ernie Chan - Algorithmic Trading, Chapter 6: Interday Momentum and Mean Reversion
Bhansali (2014) - Volatility and the Art of Risk Management
